paper.title: New stellar bow shocks and bubbles found around runaway stars

extraction.summary: The paper discusses runaway O and Be stars and searches for stellar bow shocks and bubbles around them. All objects are characterized as runaway stars (high peculiar velocity relative to their local ISM) with no discussion of possible Galactic unbound status. No objects are presented as HVS, hyper-runaway, or possibly escaping the Milky Way.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Top 10 out of 106 GOSC-Gaia DR3 runaway stars with higher E values, presented as a catalog of O-type runaway stars.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway stars with high peculiar velocities relative to their local ISM, but does not discuss any of them as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Table caption and data of GOSC-Gaia DR3 runaway stars; the paper identifies them as runaways using an E value indicating runaway nature significance, with no mention of unbound status.
Introduction defines runaway stars as stars moving with high peculiar velocity relative to their environment, with no Galactic unbound claim.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Top 10 out of 69 BeSS-Gaia DR3 runaway stars with higher E values, presented as a catalog of Be-type runaway stars.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway stars with high peculiar velocities relative to their local ISM, but does not discuss any of them as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Table caption and data of BeSS-Gaia DR3 runaway stars; the paper identifies them as runaways using an E value, with no mention of unbound status.
Introduction defines runaway stars as stars moving with high peculiar velocity relative to their environment, with no Galactic unbound claim.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Runaway stars associated with new bow shock (BS) and bubble (BU) candidates, with stellar parameters and velocities.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These are runaway stars associated with newly discovered bow shock and bubble structures. The paper discusses them in the context of ISM interaction and bow shock morphology, not as Galactic-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Section describing new bow shock and bubble candidates identified around runaway stars, with no mention of unbound or escaping status.

Table presenting stellar parameters and velocities of runaway stars associated with new bow shock candidates; velocities are peculiar velocities relative to local ISM, with no bound/unbound assessment.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Runaway stars associated with known bow shock (BS) and bubble (BU) candidates that were not characterized by previous works.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These are known runaway stars with previously identified bow shocks. The paper re-characterizes their IR geometry but does not discuss any as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Section describing known bow shock candidates among the runaway catalogs, with no mention of unbound or escaping status.
Table presenting stellar parameters and velocities of runaway stars associated with known bow shock candidates; no bound/unbound assessment is made.
